sourceMovie.title: Uncharted
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Uncharted works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Uncharted's action stayed with you, Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl (2003) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The adventure through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Uncharted excels in action, Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales (2017) doubles down on adventure to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom (1984) is the natural next step after Uncharted: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and adventure elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Mummy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Uncharted who respond to its action will find The Mummy (1999) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Armageddon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Uncharted fans keep landing on Armageddon (1998) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Tomb Raider
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Tomb Raider draws from the same action well as Uncharted, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2018 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Expendables
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Expendables (2010) captures the action appeal of Uncharted without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts The Expendables at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Uncharted — that tense edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time (2010). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: National Treasure
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind National Treasure (2004) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Uncharted rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Lost City
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lost City (2022) is the kind of film Uncharted fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Uncharted?
faq.answer: Try Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl, Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Men Tell No Tales, Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom—each shares DNA with Uncharted while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Uncharted?
faq.answer: Uncharted (2022) is best described as Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Uncharted part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Uncharted Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
